title: QA Public Overview — Canonical PASS
_fixture_purpose: Canonical PASS: all required sections present and substantively correct. overview_ref points to [119]. Executive summary present. Spine diagram contains both spines. Obstruction example has r=6, p=3, k=2. Control example lists both domains (cymatics, seismology) with shared orbit and k=2. why_it_matters present. spine_entry_points contains both [116] and [118]. PO1-PO9 all pass.
executive_summary: QA (Quantum Arithmetic) is a modular arithmetic system whose state space partitions into three orbit families — singularity, satellite, and cosmos — governed by a kernel algebra rooted in the ring Z[φ] (φ = golden ratio). The system has two independently certified public spines. The obstruction spine proves that targets r with v_p(r)=1 (p inert in Z[φ]) are completely unreachable, and that an obstruction-aware planner can prune them before search at zero cost (pruning_ratio=1.0). The control spine proves that the compiler law — orbit trajectory singularity→satellite→cosmos with path_length_k=2 — is domain-generic, holding identically across cymatics (Faraday instability) and seismology (elastic wave propagation). Both spines share the same kernel and failure algebra.

obstruction_spine.chain: [111] inert-prime arithmetic → [112] control bridge → [113] planner correctness → [114] efficiency savings → [115] synthesis cert → [116] validated report

control_spine.chain: [105] cymatics domain + [110] seismology domain → [106] compiler cert → [117] synthesis cert → [118] validated report
control_spine.theorem: orbit singularity→satellite→cosmos and path_length_k=2 preserved across all domain instances

obstruction_example.description: Target r=6, modulus m=9 (p=3, k=2). Since 3 is inert in Z[φ] and v_3(6)=1, the target is arithmetically forbidden. The obstruction-aware planner prunes r=6 before any search node is expanded: nodes_expanded=0, saved_nodes=47 (vs naive baseline), pruning_ratio=1.0. The result is certified through the full [111]→[114] chain.

control_example.description: Two physically distinct domains instantiate the same abstract orbit trajectory under the QA compiler law. Cymatics (Faraday instability): flat → stripes → hexagons, moves=[increase_amplitude, set_frequency]. Seismology (elastic wave propagation): quiet → p_wave → surface_wave, moves=[increase_gain, apply_lowpass]. Both traverse orbit singularity→satellite→cosmos with path_length_k=2. The compiler law governs abstract orbit structure; the physical domain provides only concrete instantiation.

why_it_matters: The two-spine architecture gives QA a dual character: it is simultaneously a system for proving impossibility (obstruction spine) and a system for certifying cross-domain structural equivalence (control spine). Both properties are machine-verified through a shared kernel — not asserted informally. A reviewer can verify the entire architecture by reading [116] for the obstruction story, [118] for the control story, and [119] for the unified claim. No domain expertise in cymatics or seismology is needed: the orbit structure is purely algebraic.

canonical_fail_witness.description: spine_entry_points lists only the obstruction spine, omitting the control spine
